channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok goes dark as US ban takes effect
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: “The 90-day extension is something that will be most likely done, because it's appropriate," Trump said in a call with NBC News.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: With a US ban on TikTok hours away, Trump says he 'most likely' will grant an extension
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he “most likely” will give TikTok 90 more days to work out a deal that would allow the popular video-sharing platform to avoid a U.S. ban.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump launches first official cryptocurrency
item.description: President-elect Trump launched his first official cryptocurrency, which shot up in value quickly to kick off his inauguration weekend. “My NEW Official Trump Meme is HERE! It’s time to celebrate everything we stand for: WINNING! Join my very special Trump Community.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Health care AI, intended to save money, turns out to require a lot of expensive humans
item.description: Artificial intelligence systems require consistent monitoring and staffing to put in place and to keep them working well.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: The rise
item.description: By HALELUYA HADERO The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing app. Now, the moment its fans dreaded is here, but uncertainty over TikTok’s future lingers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court’s decision on TikTok may have just turned a generation off politics
item.description: Opinions can be shaped very quickly, and grudges last a long time. Donald Trump knows that better than most. Joe Biden ought to, but seemingly doesn’t.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Apple is in the race for workplace AI
item.description: In a few years, every new employee entering the workforce will already have become accustomed to using AI to solve problems and help with tasks – and they’re going to want the same tools at work as those they use at home.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Support for Microsoft 365 on Windows 10 ends in mid-October
item.description:
Support for Microsoft 365 ends along with Windows 10 PixieMe/Shutterstock
Although Microsoft announced some time ago that Windows 10 will only be supported (free of charge) until October 14, 2025, the switch to its successor Windows 11 is only taking place slowly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Lenovo to acquire Infinidat to expand its storage folio
item.description: Lenovo Group has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tel Aviv-based enterprise storage provider Infinidat, for an undisclosed sum, to expand its storage offerings. The startup provides several offerings, such as InfiniBox, Infinibox SSA, InfiniGuard, and InfuzeOS.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Americans keep falsely claiming this random man from Vancouver is RedNote’s CEO
item.description: Americans flocking to the Chinese app RedNote thought they’d been welcomed by the platform’s CEO. Turns out, he’s just a guy from Vancouver. “It was a typical Sunday,” Jerry tells Fast Company. “I was home alone, browsing RedNote as usual.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok refugees or propagandists? China’s RedNote users share mixed responses to U.S. influx
item.description: Users of the Chinese social media app RedNote welcomed “TikTok refugees” from the United States with selfies and messages on Wednesday, as Beijing said it encouraged stronger cultural ties with other countries in response to the sudden influx.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Google inks a Gemini AI chatbot deal with the Associated Press
item.description: Google says its artificial intelligence chatbot Gemini will deliver up-to-date news from the Associated Press in the tech giant’s first such deal with a news publisher.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple satellite patent takes big step toward stable communication
item.description: Apple has big plans for satellite services, a new patent filing confirms. It’s the latest nugget of information to roll down Apple Confidential mountain, a plan to make it much easier for devices to maintain connectivity as they move between satellites.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Network convergence will drive enterprise 6G wireless strategies
item.description: The traditional separation between Wi-Fi and cellular networks — a cornerstone of enterprise wireless planning — is facing fundamental challenges according to a new 6G vision statement released this week by the World Broadband Association, “It is important that the 6G era brings together cellular, W
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A Singular Shift: Singular Bank Appoints Rubén Andrés Priego as Director General of Technology and Operations
item.description: Singular Bank has made headlines with the appointment of Rubén Andrés Priego as its new director general of technology and operations, marking a significant shift in the bank's leadership.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: HPE adelanta a Dell y Supermicro al sellar un acuerdo con X: servidores de IA por 1.000 millones de dólares
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) ha firmado un contrato superior a 1.000 millones de dólares para suministrar servidores de inteligencia artificial a X, la plataforma antes conocida como Twitter, según Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From Safety to Accountability: The Game-Changing Impact of Kazakhstan’s Surveillance System
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox will stream next month's Super Bowl for free on Tubi
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fox will stream the Super Bowl on Tubi for the first time, giving streamers and cord cutters a chance to watch the big game on Feb. 9 for free. Tubi was acquired by Fox Corporation in 2020. It is a free, ad-supported streaming service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US TikTok users flock to Chinese app Xiaohongshu in protest with TikTok ban looming
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — As the threat of a TikTok ban looms, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
